evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Implement data quality and validation processes to guarantee data accuracy and consistency.
evidence.domain_risk: Establish the KPIs and metrics that will be used by the business
evidence.collaboration_width: our tech and product teams; our marketing and product teams
evidence.data_team_maturity: Design, construct, and enhance data pipelines utilizing SQL, Python, dbt, git, and AWS services.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design, construct, and enhance data pipelines utilizing SQL, Python, dbt, git, and AWS services; Create and maintain data architecture and data models for various business domains.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Plan, execute, and evaluate experiments with our marketing and product teams.
evidence.autonomy_level: Establish and implement data requirements and analytics solutions with our tech and product teams.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design, construct, and enhance data pipelines utilizing SQL, Python, dbt, git, and AWS services
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The quote explicitly combines constructing new pipelines with enhancing existing ones, matching a mixed greenfield and fix/scale classification.
evidence.ai_role: Design, construct, and enhance data pipelines utilizing SQL, Python, dbt, git, and AWS services
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Standard data engineering tech stack with no AI tools (Copilot/Claude Code/Cursor) or GenAI infrastructure responsibilities mentioned.
evidence.testing_framing: Implement data quality and validation processes to guarantee data accuracy and consistency
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: The verb 'implement' paired with 'data quality and validation processes' signals candidate owns the quality practice, not just a tool in the stack.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Implement data quality and validation processes to guarantee data accuracy and consistency
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Quality framing focuses on delivery (accuracy, consistency) without operational reliability signals (SLOs, incidents, pipeline stability) or compliance/trust signals (regulatory, audit, bad data risk).
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The responsibilities emphasize data accuracy, consistency, and validation as core expectations, with no language signaling speed or iteration velocity.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Business-critical role but no explicit financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance language suggesting high-risk consequences.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools deployed in the responsibilities (dbt, AWS services) indicate an existing data function with infrastructure in a growth and enhancement phase, not a greenfield build.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The responsibilities contain specific technical tools, methodologies (ETL/ELT, data modeling), and precise application context that only someone with hands-on data engineering experience would articulate.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Core responsibility for planning and executing experiments is a product analytics function, and product teams are mentioned explicitly in multiple responsibilities.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role strategically establishes what to measure, architect, and implement (strategic verbs), but does so collaboratively 'with' product and engineering teams in service of their priorities (execution framing).
